subject: [PATCH v1 11/11] io_uring/cmd: set selected buffer index in __io_uring_cmd_done()

2026-02-24.patch_summary: This patch adds the ability to set the selected buffer index in the __io_uring_cmd_done() function, which is part of the io_uring command handling code. This allows the kernel to keep track of the currently selected buffer when issuing commands through the io_uring interface. The change is a part of a larger series that introduces kernel-managed buffer rings, where the kernel allocates and manages buffers on behalf of applications using io_uring. The patch builds upon previous changes in the series, including support for kernel-managed buffer rings, mmap support, and recycling of buffers.
